        "bot:mood:tired": "recentBotMood:boredtired",
        "bot:mood:drained": "recentBotMood:boredtired",


        // -----------------------------
        // OVERALL EMOTION (thread)
        // -----------------------------

        // neutral
        "overall:mood:neutral": "overallMood:neutral",

        // content_warm
        "overall:mood:content": "overallMood:contentwarm",
        "overall:mood:warm": "overallMood:contentwarm",
        "overall:mood:soft": "overallMood:contentwarm",

        // happy_joyful
        "overall:mood:happy": "overallMood:happyjoyful",
        "overall:mood:joy": "overallMood:happyjoyful",
        "overall:mood:cheerful": "overallMood:happyjoyful",

        // excited_thrilled
        "overall:mood:excited": "overallMood:excitedthrilled",
        "overall:mood:thrilled": "overallMood:excitedthrilled",
        "overall:mood:hype": "overallMood:excitedthrilled",

        // tense_anxious
        "overall:mood:tense": "overallMood:tenseanxious",
        "overall:mood:anxious": "overallMood:tenseanxious",
        "overall:mood:nervous": "overallMood:tenseanxious",

        // sad_hurt
        "overall:mood:sad": "overallMood:sadhurt",
        "overall:mood:hurt": "overallMood:sadhurt",
        "overall:mood:down": "overallMood:sadhurt",

        // angry_irritated
        "overall:mood:angry": "overallMood:angryirritated",
        "overall:mood:irritated": "overallMood:angryirritated",
        "overall:mood:annoyed": "overallMood:angryirritated",

        // bored_tired
        "overall:mood:bored": "overallMood:boredtired",
        "overall:mood:tired": "overallMood:boredtired",
        "overall:mood:drained": "overallMood:boredtired",



        // ==========================================================
        // SUBTEXT (MODEL.subtextLabels)
        // Canonical: literalonthenose, hiddenvulnerability,
        // politeindirecthint, playfulteasing, deadpanambiguous,
        // deflectiondodge, sarcasmmild, sarcasmsharp,
        // passiveaggressive, mockingderisive,
        // overthetopexaggeration, testingboundaries,
        // withholdingomission
        // ==========================================================

        // -----------------------------
        // GENERIC (speaker-agnostic)
        // -----------------------------

        // literal_on_the_nose
        "subtext:literal": "sub literalonthenose",
        "subtext:on_the_nose": "sub literalonthenose",

        // hidden_vulnerability
        "subtext:vulnerable": "sub hiddenvulnerability",
        "subtext:masking": "sub hiddenvulnerability",
        "subtext:soft_core": "sub hiddenvulnerability",

        // polite_indirect_hint
        "subtext:polite": "sub politeindirecthint",
        "subtext:hint": "sub politeindirecthint",
        "subtext:roundabout": "sub politeindirecthint",

        // playful_teasing
        "subtext:playful": "sub playfulteasing",
        "subtext:teasing": "sub playfulteasing",
        "subtext:light_tease": "sub playfulteasing",

        // deadpan_ambiguous
        "subtext:deadpan": "sub deadpanambiguous",
        "subtext:flat": "sub deadpanambiguous",
        "subtext:ambiguous": "sub deadpanambiguous",

        // deflection_dodge
        "subtext:evasive": "sub deflectiondodge",
        "subtext:deflection": "sub deflectiondodge",
        "subtext:dodging": "sub deflectiondodge",

        // sarcasm_mild
        "subtext:sarcasm": "sub sarcasmmild",
        "subtext:sarcasm_mild": "sub sarcasmmild",
        "subtext:dry": "sub sarcasmmild",

        // sarcasm_sharp
        "subtext:sarcasm_sharp": "sub sarcasmsharp",
        "subtext:snark": "sub sarcasmsharp",
        "subtext:cutting": "sub sarcasmsharp",

        // passive_aggressive
        "subtext:passive_aggressive": "sub passiveaggressive",
        "subtext:passive-aggressive": "sub passiveaggressive",
        "subtext:backhanded": "sub passiveaggressive",

        // mocking_derisive
        "subtext:mocking": "sub mockingderisive",
        "subtext:derisive": "sub mockingderisive",
        "subtext:mean_tease": "sub mockingderisive",

        // over_the_top_exaggeration
        "subtext:exaggeration": "sub overthetopexaggeration",
        "subtext:over_the_top": "sub overthetopexaggeration",
        "subtext:dramatic": "sub overthetopexaggeration",

        // testing_boundaries
        "subtext:testing": "sub testingboundaries",
        "subtext:boundaries": "sub testingboundaries",
        "subtext:probing": "sub testingboundaries",

        // withholding_omission
        "subtext:withholding": "sub withholdingomission",
        "subtext:omission": "sub withholdingomission",
        "subtext:holding_back": "sub withholdingomission",


        // -----------------------------
        // USER SUBTEXT
        // -----------------------------

        // literal_on_the_nose
        "user:subtext:literal": "recentUserSubtext:literalonthenose",
        "user:subtext:on_the_nose": "recentUserSubtext:literalonthenose",

        // hidden_vulnerability
        "user:subtext:vulnerable": "recentUserSubtext:hiddenvulnerability",
        "user:subtext:masking": "recentUserSubtext:hiddenvulnerability",
        "user:subtext:soft_core": "recentUserSubtext:hiddenvulnerability",

        // polite_indirect_hint
        "user:subtext:polite": "recentUserSubtext:politeindirecthint",
        "user:subtext:hint": "recentUserSubtext:politeindirecthint",
        "user:subtext:roundabout": "recentUserSubtext:politeindirecthint",

        // playful_teasing
        "user:subtext:playful": "recentUserSubtext:playfulteasing",
        "user:subtext:teasing": "recentUserSubtext:playfulteasing",
        "user:subtext:light_tease": "recentUserSubtext:playfulteasing",

        // deadpan_ambiguous
        "user:subtext:deadpan": "recentUserSubtext:deadpanambiguous",
        "user:subtext:flat": "recentUserSubtext:deadpanambiguous",
        "user:subtext:ambiguous": "recentUserSubtext:deadpanambiguous",

        // deflection_dodge
        "user:subtext:evasive": "recentUserSubtext:deflectiondodge",
        "user:subtext:deflection": "recentUserSubtext:deflectiondodge",
        "user:subtext:dodging": "recentUserSubtext:deflectiondodge",

        // sarcasm_mild
        "user:subtext:sarcasm": "recentUserSubtext:sarcasmmild",
        "user:subtext:sarcasm_mild": "recentUserSubtext:sarcasmmild",
        "user:subtext:dry": "recentUserSubtext:sarcasmmild",

        // sarcasm_sharp
        "user:subtext:sarcasm_sharp": "recentUserSubtext:sarcasmsharp",
        "user:subtext:snark": "recentUserSubtext:sarcasmsharp",
        "user:subtext:cutting": "recentUserSubtext:sarcasmsharp",

        // passive_aggressive
        "user:subtext:passive_aggressive": "recentUserSubtext:passiveaggressive",
        "user:subtext:passive-aggressive": "recentUserSubtext:passiveaggressive",
        "user:subtext:backhanded": "recentUserSubtext:passiveaggressive",

        // mocking_derisive
        "user:subtext:mocking": "recentUserSubtext:mockingderisive",
        "user:subtext:derisive": "recentUserSubtext:mockingderisive",
        "user:subtext:mean_tease": "recentUserSubtext:mockingderisive",

        // over_the_top_exaggeration
        "user:subtext:exaggeration": "recentUserSubtext:overthetopexaggeration",
        "user:subtext:over_the_top": "recentUserSubtext:overthetopexaggeration",
        "user:subtext:dramatic": "recentUserSubtext:overthetopexaggeration",

        // testing_boundaries
        "user:subtext:testing": "recentUserSubtext:testingboundaries",
        "user:subtext:boundaries": "recentUserSubtext:testingboundaries",
        "user:subtext:probing": "recentUserSubtext:testingboundaries",

        // withholding_omission
        "user:subtext:withholding": "recentUserSubtext:withholdingomission",
        "user:subtext:omission": "recentUserSubtext:withholdingomission",
        "user:subtext:holding_back": "recentUserSubtext:withholdingomission",


        // -----------------------------
        // BOT SUBTEXT
        // -----------------------------

        // literal_on_the_nose
        "bot:subtext:literal": "recentBotSubtext:literalonthenose",
        "bot:subtext:on_the_nose": "recentBotSubtext:literalonthenose",

        // hidden_vulnerability
        "bot:subtext:vulnerable": "recentBotSubtext:hiddenvulnerability",
        "bot:subtext:masking": "recentBotSubtext:hiddenvulnerability",
        "bot:subtext:soft_core": "recentBotSubtext:hiddenvulnerability",

        // polite_indirect_hint
        "bot:subtext:polite": "recentBotSubtext:politeindirecthint",
        "bot:subtext:hint": "recentBotSubtext:politeindirecthint",
        "bot:subtext:roundabout": "recentBotSubtext:politeindirecthint",

        // playful_teasing
        "bot:subtext:playful": "recentBotSubtext:playfulteasing",
        "bot:subtext:teasing": "recentBotSubtext:playfulteasing",
        "bot:subtext:light_tease": "recentBotSubtext:playfulteasing",

        // deadpan_ambiguous
        "bot:subtext:deadpan": "recentBotSubtext:deadpanambiguous",
        "bot:subtext:flat": "recentBotSubtext:deadpanambiguous",
        "bot:subtext:ambiguous": "recentBotSubtext:deadpanambiguous",

        // deflection_dodge
        "bot:subtext:evasive": "recentBotSubtext:deflectiondodge",
        "bot:subtext:deflection": "recentBotSubtext:deflectiondodge",
        "bot:subtext:dodging": "recentBotSubtext:deflectiondodge",

        // sarcasm_mild
        "bot:subtext:sarcasm": "recentBotSubtext:sarcasmmild",
        "bot:subtext:sarcasm_mild": "recentBotSubtext:sarcasmmild",
        "bot:subtext:dry": "recentBotSubtext:sarcasmmild",

        // sarcasm_sharp
        "bot:subtext:sarcasm_sharp": "recentBotSubtext:sarcasmsharp",
        "bot:subtext:snark": "recentBotSubtext:sarcasmsharp",
        "bot:subtext:cutting": "recentBotSubtext:sarcasmsharp",

        // passive_aggressive
        "bot:subtext:passive_aggressive": "recentBotSubtext:passiveaggressive",
        "bot:subtext:passive-aggressive": "recentBotSubtext:passiveaggressive",
        "bot:subtext:backhanded": "recentBotSubtext:passiveaggressive",

        // mocking_derisive
        "bot:subtext:mocking": "recentBotSubtext:mockingderisive",
        "bot:subtext:derisive": "recentBotSubtext:mockingderisive",
        "bot:subtext:mean_tease": "recentBotSubtext:mockingderisive",

        // over_the_top_exaggeration
        "bot:subtext:exaggeration": "recentBotSubtext:overthetopexaggeration",
        "bot:subtext:over_the_top": "recentBotSubtext:overthetopexaggeration",
        "bot:subtext:dramatic": "recentBotSubtext:overthetopexaggeration",

        // testing_boundaries
        "bot:subtext:testing": "recentBotSubtext:testingboundaries",
        "bot:subtext:boundaries": "recentBotSubtext:testingboundaries",
        "bot:subtext:probing": "recentBotSubtext:testingboundaries",

        // withholding_omission
        "bot:subtext:withholding": "recentBotSubtext:withholdingomission",
        "bot:subtext:omission": "recentBotSubtext:withholdingomission",
        "bot:subtext:holding_back": "recentBotSubtext:withholdingomission",


        // -----------------------------
        // OVERALL SUBTEXT (thread)
        // -----------------------------

        // literal_on_the_nose
        "overall:subtext:literal": "overallSubtext:literalonthenose",
        "overall:subtext:on_the_nose": "overallSubtext:literalonthenose",

        // hidden_vulnerability
        "overall:subtext:vulnerable": "overallSubtext:hiddenvulnerability",
        "overall:subtext:masking": "overallSubtext:hiddenvulnerability",
        "overall:subtext:soft_core": "overallSubtext:hiddenvulnerability",

        // polite_indirect_hint
        "overall:subtext:polite": "overallSubtext:politeindirecthint",
        "overall:subtext:hint": "overallSubtext:politeindirecthint",
        "overall:subtext:roundabout": "overallSubtext:politeindirecthint",

        // playful_teasing
        "overall:subtext:playful": "overallSubtext:playfulteasing",
        "overall:subtext:teasing": "overallSubtext:playfulteasing",
        "overall:subtext:light_tease": "overallSubtext:playfulteasing",

        // deadpan_ambiguous
        "overall:subtext:deadpan": "overallSubtext:deadpanambiguous",
        "overall:subtext:flat": "overallSubtext:deadpanambiguous",
        "overall:subtext:ambiguous": "overallSubtext:deadpanambiguous",

        // deflection_dodge
        "overall:subtext:evasive": "overallSubtext:deflectiondodge",
        "overall:subtext:deflection": "overallSubtext:deflectiondodge",
        "overall:subtext:dodging": "overallSubtext:deflectiondodge",

        // sarcasm_mild
        "overall:subtext:sarcasm": "overallSubtext:sarcasmmild",
        "overall:subtext:sarcasm_mild": "overallSubtext:sarcasmmild",
        "overall:subtext:dry": "overallSubtext:sarcasmmild",

        // sarcasm_sharp
        "overall:subtext:sarcasm_sharp": "overallSubtext:sarcasmsharp",
        "overall:subtext:snark": "overallSubtext:sarcasmsharp",
        "overall:subtext:cutting": "overallSubtext:sarcasmsharp",

        // passive_aggressive
        "overall:subtext:passive_aggressive": "overallSubtext:passiveaggressive",
        "overall:subtext:passive-aggressive": "overallSubtext:passiveaggressive",
        "overall:subtext:backhanded": "overallSubtext:passiveaggressive",

        // mocking_derisive
        "overall:subtext:mocking": "overallSubtext:mockingderisive",
        "overall:subtext:derisive": "overallSubtext:mockingderisive",
        "overall:subtext:mean_tease": "overallSubtext:mockingderisive",

        // over_the_top_exaggeration
        "overall:subtext:exaggeration": "overallSubtext:overthetopexaggeration",
        "overall:subtext:over_the_top": "overallSubtext:overthetopexaggeration",
        "overall:subtext:dramatic": "overallSubtext:overthetopexaggeration",

        // testing_boundaries
        "overall:subtext:testing": "overallSubtext:testingboundaries",
        "overall:subtext:boundaries": "overallSubtext:testingboundaries",
        "overall:subtext:probing": "overallSubtext:testingboundaries",

        // withholding_omission
        "overall:subtext:withholding": "overallSubtext:withholdingomission",
        "overall:subtext:omission": "overallSubtext:withholdingomission",
        "overall:subtext:holding_back": "overallSubtext:withholdingomission"
    };

    /* ============================================================================
       [SECTION] OUTPUT GUARDS
       ========================================================================== */
    context.character.personality = (typeof context.character.personality === "string")
        ? context.character.personality : "";
    context.character.scenario = (typeof context.character.scenario === "string")
        ? context.character.scenario : "";

    /* ============================================================================
       [SECTION] INPUT NORMALIZATION (PRECISION REWORK)
       ========================================================================== */
    var WINDOW_DEPTH = 5;

    function _str(x) { return (x == null ? "" : String(x)); }
    function _normalizeText(s) {
        s = _str(s).toLowerCase();
        s = s.replace(/[^a-z0-9_\s-]/g, " ");
        s = s.replace(/[-_]+/g, " ");
        s = s.replace(/\s+/g, " ").trim();
        return s;
    }

    // --- Message Extraction Logic ---
    var _lmArr = (context && context.chat && context.chat.last_messages && typeof context.chat.last_messages.length === "number")
        ? context.chat.last_messages : null;

    var _joinedWindow = "";
    var _rawLastSingle = "";

    // Specific Focus Variables for CNN
    var TXT_USER_FOCUS = "";   // Last message (Index -1)
    var TXT_BOT_FOCUS = "";   // 2nd to last message (Index -2)
    var TXT_SCENE_FOCUS = "";  // Last 6 messages

    if (_lmArr && _lmArr.length > 0) {
        var L__ = _lmArr.length;

        function _msgAt(idx) {
            if (idx < 0 || idx >= L__) return "";
            var it = _lmArr[idx];
            return (it && typeof it.message === "string") ? it.message : _str(it);
        }

        // 1. Text for keyword matching (Word Gates)
        var startIdx = Math.max(0, L__ - WINDOW_DEPTH);
        var segs = [];
        for (var i = startIdx; i < L__; i++) {
            segs.push(_msgAt(i));
        }
        _joinedWindow = segs.join(" ");
        _rawLastSingle = _msgAt(L__ - 1);

        // 2. Text for CNN Targeting
        // USER FOCUS: Strictly the very last message (Index -1)
        if (L__ >= 1) {
            TXT_USER_FOCUS = _msgAt(L__ - 1);
        }

        // BOT FOCUS: Strictly the message before last (Index -2)
        if (L__ >= 2) {
            TXT_BOT_FOCUS = _msgAt(L__ - 2);
        }

        // SCENE FOCUS: Last 6 messages (Index -6 to -1)
        var sceneSegs = [];
        var startSix = Math.max(0, L__ - 6);
        for (var si = startSix; si < L__; si++) {
            sceneSegs.push(_msgAt(si));
        }
        TXT_SCENE_FOCUS = sceneSegs.join(" ");

    } else {
        // Fallback for empty/single
        var _lastMsgA = (context && context.chat && typeof context.chat.lastMessage === "string") ? context.chat.lastMessage : "";
        var _lastMsgB = (context && context.chat && typeof context.chat.last_message === "string") ? context.chat.last_message : "";
        _rawLastSingle = _str(_lastMsgA || _lastMsgB);
        _joinedWindow = _rawLastSingle;
        TXT_USER_FOCUS = _rawLastSingle; // Assume user started it
        TXT_SCENE_FOCUS = _rawLastSingle;
    }

    /* ============================================================================
       [SECTION] CNN INTEGRATION: PRECISE TARGETING
       ========================================================================== */
    var CNN_KEYWORDS = "";
    var CNN_USER_KEYWORDS = "";
    var CNN_BOT_KEYWORDS = "";

    (function () {
        try {
            if (typeof cnn_predict !== "function") return;

            // --- RUN CNN ON DISCRETE BLOCKS ---
            // 1. User Emotional State (1 message ago)
            var resUser = TXT_USER_FOCUS ? cnn_predict(TXT_USER_FOCUS) : null;

            // 2. Bot Last Mood (2nd message back)
            var resBot = TXT_BOT_FOCUS ? cnn_predict(TXT_BOT_FOCUS) : null;

            // 3. Overall Scene (Last 6)
            var resScene = TXT_SCENE_FOCUS ? cnn_predict(TXT_SCENE_FOCUS) : null;

            var charName = (context.character && typeof context.character.name === "string")
                ? context.character.name : "{{char}}";

            // --- SCENARIO OUTPUT: STRICT SEPARATION ---
            if (resUser && typeof cnn_formatResult === "function") {
                var prettyU = cnn_formatResult(resUser);
                if (prettyU) context.character.scenario += "\n\n[User (Latest Message)]\n" + prettyU;
            }

            if (resBot && typeof cnn_formatResult === "function") {
                var prettyB = cnn_formatResult(resBot);
                if (prettyB) context.character.scenario += "\n\n[" + charName + " (Previous Message)]\n" + prettyB;
            }

            if (resScene && typeof cnn_formatResult === "function") {
                var prettyS = cnn_formatResult(resScene);
                if (prettyS) context.character.scenario += "\n\n[Scene (Last 6 Messages)]\n" + prettyS;
            }

            if (!MODEL) return;

            var kwList = [];

            function getBestEmo(res) {
                if (!res || !res.emotionProbs || !MODEL.emotionLabels) return null;
                var bestI = -1, bestP = 0;
                for (var i = 0; i < MODEL.emotionLabels.length; i++) {
                    if (res.emotionProbs[i] > bestP) { bestP = res.emotionProbs[i]; bestI = i; }
                }
                if (bestI >= 0 && bestP >= 0.35) return cnn_labelToWord(MODEL.emotionLabels[bestI]);
                return null;
            }

            function getSubtexts(res) {
                if (!res || !res.subtextProbs || !MODEL.subtextLabels) return [];
                var out = [];
                for (var i = 0; i < MODEL.subtextLabels.length; i++) {
                    if (res.subtextProbs[i] >= 0.55) {
                        var w = cnn_labelToWord(MODEL.subtextLabels[i]);
                        if (w) out.push(w);
                    }
                }
                return out;
            }

            // --- KEYWORD GENERATION ---
            // "User" keywords come ONLY from resUser (Index -1)
            var uEmo = getBestEmo(resUser);
            if (uEmo) {
                kwList.push("recentUserMood:" + uEmo);
                kwList.push("userMood:" + uEmo);
                kwList.push("emo " + uEmo);
            }
            var uSubs = getSubtexts(resUser);
            for (var ui = 0; ui < uSubs.length; ui++) {
                kwList.push("recentUserSubtext:" + uSubs[ui]);
                kwList.push("userSubtext:" + uSubs[ui]);
                kwList.push("sub " + uSubs[ui]);
            }

            // "Bot" keywords come ONLY from resBot (Index -2)
            var bEmo = getBestEmo(resBot);
            if (bEmo) {
                kwList.push("recentBotMood:" + bEmo);
                kwList.push("botMood:" + bEmo);
                kwList.push("bemo " + bEmo);
            }
            var bSubs = getSubtexts(resBot);
            for (var bi = 0; bi < bSubs.length; bi++) {
                kwList.push("recentBotSubtext:" + bSubs[bi]);
                kwList.push("botSubtext:" + bSubs[bi]);
                kwList.push("bsub " + bSubs[bi]);
            }

            // (Optional) Scene keywords could be added here if you want "sceneMood:tense"
            // based on resScene, but standard logic usually relies on specific actors.

            CNN_KEYWORDS = kwList.join(" ");
            CNN_USER_KEYWORDS = kwList.filter(function (k) { return k.indexOf("User") !== -1; }).join(" ");
            CNN_BOT_KEYWORDS = kwList.filter(function (k) { return k.indexOf("Bot") !== -1; }).join(" ");

        } catch (e) {
            // Fail closed
        }
    })();

    /* ============================================================================
       [SECTION] CHAT_WINDOW + HAYSTACK
       ========================================================================== */
    var CHAT_WINDOW = {
        depth: WINDOW_DEPTH,
        text_joined_norm: _normalizeText(_joinedWindow)
    };

    // This is the string the lore engine searches with hasTerm(...)
    var last = " " + CHAT_WINDOW.text_joined_norm + " " + _normalizeText(CNN_KEYWORDS) + " ";

    // --- Message count -----------------------------------------------------------
    var messageCount = 0;
    if (_lmArr && typeof _lmArr.length === "number") {
        messageCount = _lmArr.length;
    } else if (context && context.chat && typeof context.chat.message_count === "number") {
        messageCount = context.chat.message_count;
    } else if (typeof context_chat_message_count === "number") {
        messageCount = context_chat_message_count;
    }

    // --- Active character name ---------------------------------------------------
    var activeName = _normalizeText(
        (context && context.character && typeof context.character.name === "string")
            ? context.character.name : ""
    );

    /* ============================================================================
       [SECTION] UTILITIES
       ========================================================================== */
    function dbg(msg) {
        try {
            if (typeof DEBUG !== "undefined" && DEBUG) {
                context.character.personality += "\n\n[DBG] " + String(msg);
            }
        } catch (e) { }
    }
    function arr(x) { return Array.isArray(x) ? x : (x == null ? [] : [x]); }
    function clamp01(v) { v = +v; if (!isFinite(v)) return 0; return Math.max(0, Math.min(1, v)); }
    function parseProbability(v) {
        if (v == null) return 1;
        if (typeof v === "number") return clamp01(v);
        var s = String(v).trim().toLowerCase();
        var n = parseFloat(s.replace("%", ""));
        if (!isFinite(n)) return 1;
        return s.indexOf("%") !== -1 ? clamp01(n / 100) : clamp01(n);
    }
    function prio(e) {
        var p = (e && isFinite(e.priority)) ? +e.priority : 3;
        if (p < 1) p = 1;
        if (p > 5) p = 5;
        return p;
    }
    function getMin(e) { return (e && isFinite(e.minMessages)) ? +e.minMessages : -Infinity; }
    function getMax(e) { return (e && isFinite(e.maxMessages)) ? +e.maxMessages : Infinity; }
    function getKW(e) { return (e && Array.isArray(e.keywords)) ? e.keywords.slice(0) : []; }
    function getTrg(e) { return (e && Array.isArray(e.triggers)) ? e.triggers.slice(0) : []; }
    function getBlk(e) {
        if (!e) return [];
        if (Array.isArray(e.block)) return e.block.slice(0);
        if (Array.isArray(e.Block)) return e.Block.slice(0);
        return [];
    }
    function getNameBlock(e) { return (e && Array.isArray(e.nameBlock)) ? e.nameBlock.slice(0) : []; }
    function normName(s) { return _normalizeText(s); }

    function isNameBlocked(e) {
        if (!activeName) return false;

        var nb = getNameBlock(e);
        for (var i = 0; i < nb.length; i++) {
            var n = normName(nb[i]);
            if (!n) continue;

            // direct match
            if (n === activeName) return true;

            // substring match
            if (activeName.indexOf(n) !== -1) return true;

            // name starts with
            if (n.indexOf(activeName + " ") === 0) return true;
        }

        return false;
    }


    function reEsc(s) { return String(s).replace(/[.*+?^${}()|[\]\\]/g, "\\$&"); }

    function hasTerm(hay, term) {
        var t = (term == null ? "" : String(term)).toLowerCase().trim();
        if (!t) return false;
        if (t.charAt(t.length - 1) === "*") {
            var stem = reEsc(t.slice(0, -1));
            var re1 = new RegExp("(?:^|\\s)" + stem + "[a-z]*?(?=\\s|$)");
            return re1.test(hay);
        }
        var w = reEsc(t);
        var re2 = new RegExp("(?:^|\\s)" + w + "(?=\\s|$)");
        return re2.test(hay);
    }

    function expandAlias(term) {
        return CNN_ALIAS[term] ? CNN_ALIAS[term] : term;
    }

    function collectWordGates(e) {
        var r = (e && e.requires) ? e.requires : {};
        var any = [].concat(arr(e && e.requireAny), arr(e && e.andAny), arr(r.any));
        var all = [].concat(arr(e && e.requireAll), arr(e && e.andAll), arr(r.all));
        var none = [].concat(arr(e && e.requireNone), arr(e && e.notAny), arr(r.none), arr(getBlk(e)));
        var nall = [].concat(arr(e && e.notAll));
        return { any: any, all: all, none: none, nall: nall };
    }

    function wordGatesPass(e) {
        var g = collectWordGates(e);
        if (g.any.length && !g.any.some(function (w) { return hasTerm(last, w); })) return false;
        if (g.all.length && !g.all.every(function (w) { return hasTerm(last, w); })) return false;
        if (g.none.length && g.none.some(function (w) { return hasTerm(last, w); })) return false;
        if (g.nall.length && g.nall.every(function (w) { return hasTerm(last, w); })) return false;
        return true;
    }

    function tagsPass(e, activeTagsSet) {
        var anyT = arr(e && e.andAnyTags);
        var allT = arr(e && e.andAllTags);
        var noneT = arr(e && e.notAnyTags);
        var nallT = arr(e && e.notAllTags);
        var hasT = function (t) { return !!activeTagsSet && activeTagsSet[String(t)] === 1; };

        if (anyT.length && !anyT.some(hasT)) return false;
        if (allT.length && !allT.every(hasT)) return false;
        if (noneT.length && noneT.some(hasT)) return false;
        if (nallT.length && nallT.every(hasT)) return false;
        return true;
    }

    function isAlwaysOn(e) {
        var hasKW = !!(e && e.keywords && e.keywords.length);
        var hasTag = !!(e && e.tag);
        var hasMin = (e && e.minMessages != null);
        var hasMax = (e && e.maxMessages != null);
        return !hasKW && !hasTag && !hasMin && !hasMax;
    }

    function entryPasses(e, activeTagsSet) {
        if (!(messageCount >= getMin(e) && messageCount <= getMax(e))) return false;
        if (isNameBlocked(e)) return false;
        if (!wordGatesPass(e)) return false;
        if (!tagsPass(e, activeTagsSet || {})) return false;
        if (Math.random() > parseProbability(e && e.probability)) return false;
        return true;
    }
    /* ============================================================================
   [SECTION] NEW GATING HELPERS (add near the other helper functions)
   ========================================================================= */

    function getSpeaker() {
        if (!context || !context.chat || !context.chat.length) return null;
        const lastMsg = context.chat[context.chat.length - 1];
        return lastMsg.isUser ? "user" : "char";
    }

    function countKeywordOccurrences(term) {
        if (!context || !context.chat) return 0;
        let count = 0;
        const normTerm = " " + term.toLowerCase() + " ";
        for (let i = 0; i < context.chat.length; i++) {
            if (hasTerm(context.chat[i].text, term)) count++;
        }
        return count;
    }

    function keywordInRecency(term, n) {
        if (!context || !context.chat || context.chat.length < 1) return false;
        const start = Math.max(0, context.chat.length - n);
        for (let i = start; i < context.chat.length; i++) {
            if (hasTerm(context.chat[i].text, term)) return true;
        }
        return false;
    }

    function keywordAtDepth(term, minDepth = 0, maxDepth = Infinity) {
        if (!context || !context.chat) return false;
        for (let i = 0; i < context.chat.length; i++) {
            if (hasTerm(context.chat[i].text, term)) {
                const depth = context.chat.length - 1 - i;
                if (depth >= minDepth && depth <= maxDepth) return true;
            }
        }
        return false;
    }

    // Smart cooldown using deterministic hash of recent context
    function passesCooldown(e) {
        if (!e.cooldown) return true;
        const recent = (last || "") + (context.chat.slice(-4).map(m => m.text).join(""));
        let hash = 0;
        for (let i = 0; i < recent.length; i++) hash = ((hash << 5) - hash + recent.charCodeAt(i)) | 0;
        return (hash & 0x7FFFFFFF) % (e.cooldown + 5) !== 1;  // ~1 in (cooldown+5) chance to fire immediately again
    }

    /* ============================================================================
       UPDATED: getKW() – now understands {any/all} syntax
       ========================================================================= */
    function getKW(e) {
        if (!e) return [];
        if (Array.isArray(e.keywords)) return e.keywords;
        if (e.keywords && typeof e.keywords === "object") {
            return {
                any: Array.isArray(e.keywords.any) ? e.keywords.any : [],
                all: Array.isArray(e.keywords.all) ? e.keywords.all : []
            };
        }
        return [];
    }

    /* ============================================================================
       UPDATED: wordGatesPass() – now handles new logic
       ========================================================================= */
    function wordGatesPass(e) {
        const kw = getKW(e);
        let hasAny = false;
        let missingAll = false;

        if (Array.isArray(kw)) {
            // Legacy mode – simple OR
            if (kw.length && !kw.some(function (w) { return hasTerm(last, w); })) return false;
        } else {
            // New AND/OR mode
            if (kw.any.length) {
                hasAny = kw.any.some(function (w) { return hasTerm(last, w); });
                if (!hasAny) return false;
            }
            if (kw.all.length) {
                missingAll = kw.all.some(function (w) { return !hasTerm(last, w); });
                if (missingAll) return false;
            }
            if (!kw.any.length && !kw.all.length) return false; // need at least one
        }

        // Recency / Depth / Frequency
        const terms = Array.isArray(kw) ? kw : [...kw.any, ...kw.all];
        if (e.recency !== undefined) {
            const ok = terms.some(t => keywordInRecency(t, e.recency));
            if (!ok) return false;
        }
        if (e.frequency !== undefined) {
            const ok = terms.some(t => countKeywordOccurrences(t) >= e.frequency);
            if (!ok) return false;
        }
        if (e.depthMin !== undefined || e.depthMax !== undefined) {
            const minD = e.depthMin || 0;
            const maxD = e.depthMax !== undefined ? e.depthMax : Infinity;
            const ok = terms.some(t => keywordAtDepth(t, minD, maxD));
            if (!ok) return false;
        }

        // Speaker gate
        if (e.speaker) {
            if (getSpeaker() !== e.speaker) return false;
        }

        // Cooldown
        if (!passesCooldown(e)) return false;

        // Existing gates (requireAny/requireNone/etc) remain unchanged lower down
        const g = e;
        if (g.any && g.any.length && !g.any.some(function (w) { return hasTerm(last, w); })) return false;
        if (g.all && g.all.length && !g.all.every(function (w) { return hasTerm(last, w); })) return false;
        if (g.none && g.none.length && g.none.some(function (w) { return hasTerm(last, w); })) return false;
        if (g.nall && g.nall.length && g.nall.every(function (w) { return hasTerm(last, w); })) return false;
        return true;
    }

    /* ============================================================================
       [SECTION] AUTHOR ENTRIES (dynamicLore)
       ========================================================================== */

    var dynamicLore = [
        // 🟢🟢🟢 SAFE TO EDIT BELOW THIS LINE 🟢🟢🟢

        /* L0 — Always-on demo
           What it does: Fires every turn because there are no keywords, no time gates, and no tag.
           Why use: Bootstrap a baseline voice or a harmless always-on nudge.
        */
        { personality: " This entry will always fire." },

        /* L1 — Basics: greeting keywords
           New tools: simple keyword list.
           Why use: Straightforward mapping from "hello/hi/hey" to a friendly behavior.
        */
        {
            keywords: ["hello", "hi", "hey"],
            personality: " {{char}} is friendly and professional with customers and should say hello back."
        },

        /* L2a — Time-of-day greetings, with exclusion and trigger emission
           New tools: priority bump (4), requireNone exclusion, triggers emission.
           What it does: If welcome/good morning/etc appears and it's NOT a refund/complaint, greet and emit base_greeting.
           Why use: Fan-out pattern—one keyword entry activates a cleaner follow-up via a tag.
        */
        {
            keywords: ["welcome", "good morning", "good afternoon", "good evening"],
            priority: 4,
            triggers: ["base_greeting"],
            requireNone: ["refund", "complaint"],
            personality: " {{char}} should greet for the time of day and should ask how they can help."
        },

        /* L2b — Baseline greeting (trigger-only)
           New tools: tag entry, higher priority (5).
           What it does: Fires only if 'base_greeting' tag is present.
           Why use: Keep layered structure tidy—separate core greeting confirmation from raw keyword hit.
        */
        {
            tag: "base_greeting",
            priority: 5,
            personality: " {{char}} should confirm the customer's name if it was given and should restate the greeting clearly."
        },

        /* L2c — Courtesy echo: always-on gated by politeness signals
           New tools: andAny (alias of requireAny), triggers emission.
           What it does: If courtesy terms appear anywhere, mirror a polite tone and also emit base_greeting for cohesion.
           Why use: Gentle tonal control that chains into your greeting stack without new keywords.
        */
        {
            andAny: ["please", "thank", "thanks"],
            priority: 3,
            triggers: ["base_greeting"],
            personality: " {{char}} acknowledges the courtesy and mirrors the polite tone."
        },

        /* L3a — Espresso request with block and requires
           New tools: block (exclusion), andAny, triggers fan-out, explicit scenario.
           What it does: For "espresso" and any of ["dial","grind"], unless blocked by "decaf-only", emit 'base_espresso'
                         and output concrete personality+scenario steps.
           Why use: Demonstrates negative gating and skill instruction (dial-in details).
        */
        {
            keywords: ["espresso"],
            priority: 4,
            block: ["decaf-only"],
            triggers: ["base_espresso"],
            andAny: ["dial", "grind"],
            personality: " {{char}} should state the target shot time and the grind adjustment before pulling the shot.",
            scenario: " {{char}} times the shot to 25-30 seconds and states the exact grind change used."
        },

        /* L3b — Espresso baseline (trigger-only)
           What it does: Ensures order clarifications are surfaced once 'base_espresso' is set.
           Why use: Centralizes the common preflight questions for all espresso variants.
        */
        {
            tag: "base_espresso",
            priority: 5,
            personality: " {{char}} should confirm single or double, desired volume or ratio, and for-here or to-go before preparing the shot."
        },

        /* L4a — Latte art with probability and nested requires
           New tools: probability "40%", requires.any + requires.none, triggers.
           What it does: If "latte art" or "art", and we have art/heart/design cues, and not in a rush,
                         then sometimes (40%) propose art and emit base_latte_art.
           Why use: Teaches controlled randomness and queue-aware behavior.
        */
        {
            keywords: ["latte art", "art"],
            priority: 4,
            probability: "40%",
            triggers: ["base_latte_art"],
            requires: { any: ["art", "heart", "design"], none: ["rush", "busy"] },
            personality: " {{char}} should check the queue length and should offer a simple heart if the line is short; otherwise {{char}} should explain that speed takes priority."
        },

        /* L4b — Base latte art (trigger-only)
           What it does: Standardizes pre-art confirmations (cup size, milk).
           Why use: Keeps your latte art flow consistent and centrally adjustable.
        */
        {
            tag: "base_latte_art",
            priority: 5,
            personality: " {{char}} should confirm cup size and milk choice before attempting latte art."
        },

        /* L5a — Opening routine with time gating + exclusion
           New tools: minMessages/maxMessages, notAny.
           What it does: Only in the first 3 messages (0..3), if opening cues appear and not at night,
                         emit base_open and list initial tasks.
           Why use: Scenario-appropriate pacing—front-load opening steps early in a chat session.
        */
        {
            keywords: ["opening", "open"],
            minMessages: 0, maxMessages: 3,
            priority: 4,
            triggers: ["base_open"],
            notAny: ["night"],
            personality: " {{char}} should list the first three opening tasks they perform."
        },

        /* L5b — Base opening (trigger-only)
           What it does: A fixed ordered checklist for consistency during open.
           Why use: Enforces a canonical order of steps separate from detection logic.
        */
        {
            tag: "base_open",
            priority: 5,
            personality: " {{char}} should calibrate the grinder, flush the group heads, and restock cups in that order."
        },

        /* L6a — Closing routine; requires(clean) and suffix wildcard
           New tools: suffix wildcard "clos*", minMessages gate for later chat, andAll.
           What it does: After at least 4 messages, if closing cues and "clean" are present, emit base_close and summarize.
           Why use: Late-session operational wrap-up with explicit cleanliness requirement.
        */
        {
            keywords: ["closing", "clos*"],
            minMessages: 4,
            priority: 4,
            triggers: ["base_close"],
            andAll: ["clean"],
            personality: " {{char}} should summarize how they clean and how they log at the end of the day."
        },

        /* L6b — Base closing (trigger-only)
           What it does: Standard close checklist.
           Why use: Codifies the close routine that other entries can build on.
        */
        {
            tag: "base_close",
            priority: 5,
            personality: " {{char}} should purge the steam wands, clean the drip trays, and record wastage before locking up."
        },

        /* L7a — Inventory with multiple triggers and nested requires
           New tools: multiple triggers in one entry; requires.any + requires.none bundle.
           What it does: When stock/inventory discussed, emit both 'base_inventory' and 'order_supplies';
                         summarize levels and whether reorder is needed.
           Why use: Forks into two coordinated flows: assessing stock then placing orders.
        */
        {
            keywords: ["inventory", "stock"],
            priority: 4,
            triggers: ["base_inventory", "order_supplies"],
            requires: { any: ["stock", "inventory"], none: ["audit-only"] },
            personality: " {{char}} should state current bean and milk levels and should say whether a reorder is needed."
        },

        /* L7b — Base inventory (trigger-only)
           What it does: Prompts a check and heuristic planning for tomorrow.
           Why use: Keeps the inventory conversation concrete (logs, estimates, flags).
        */
        {
            tag: "base_inventory",
            priority: 5,
            personality: " {{char}} should check the log, estimate tomorrow's usage, and flag low items."
        },

        /* L7c — Order supplies (trigger-only)
           What it does: Converts assessment into explicit quantities and an action (PO).
           Why use: Ensures conversations end with a clear operational decision.
        */
        {
            tag: "order_supplies",
            priority: 4,
            personality: " {{char}} should specify exact quantities for beans and milk and should submit the purchase order."
        },

        /* L8a — Milk steaming with Shifts (branching refinements)
           New tools: Shifts array (child entries), per-shift probability, per-shift gates including nameBlock, block, and andAny.
           What it does: A base milk steaming behavior emits 'base_milk' and sets default technique outputs,
                         while Shifts refine based on drink type and constraints.
           Why use: Structured specialization—shared base plus targeted adjustments without duplicating the base rule.
        */
        {
            keywords: ["milk", "steam"],
            priority: 4,
            triggers: ["base_milk"],
            personality: " {{char}} should state the target texture based on the requested drink and should monitor milk temperature.",
            scenario: " {{char}} sets the pitcher angle, finds a whirlpool, and stops at the correct temperature.",
            Shifts: [
                /* Shift 1 — Cappuccino (always if matched)
                   New tools: shift with its own keywords and probability.
                   Why use: Guarantees classic cappuccino foam profile when requested.
                */
                {
                    keywords: ["cappuccino"],
                    probability: 1.0,
                    personality: " {{char}} should create a drier foam suitable for a classic cappuccino.",
                    scenario: " {{char}} keeps the foam airy and maintains a stable cap."
                },
                /* Shift 2 — Latte (subsampled, avoids rush/busy)
                   New tools: probability 0.7, notAny exclusion inside a shift.
                   Why use: Offers microfoam and art if pace allows; defers when busy.
                */
                {
                    keywords: ["latte"],
                    probability: 0.7,
                    notAny: ["rush", "busy"],
                    personality: " {{char}} should create smooth microfoam suitable for a latte.",
                    scenario: " {{char}} aims for a glossy texture that allows simple latte art."
                },
                /* Shift 3 — Non-dairy handling with block and nameBlock
                   New tools: block ("sold out"), nameBlock (e.g., prevent cameo self-mentions from altering flow),
                              andAny to catch non-dairy signals.
                   Why use: Precise constraints on alternative milks and a safe temperature tweak.
                */
                {
                    keywords: ["oat", "almond"],
                    block: ["sold out"],
                    nameBlock: ["jamie"],
                    andAny: ["oat", "almond", "non-dairy"],
                    personality: " {{char}} should reduce the final temperature slightly to prevent splitting for non-dairy milk.",
                    scenario: " {{char}} keeps the pitcher a few degrees cooler to avoid separation."
                }
            ]
        },

        /* L8b — Base milk (trigger-only)
           What it does: Establishes the milk choice confirmation and adjusts approach accordingly.
           Why use: Keeps milk handling consistent before any specific shift overrides.
        */
        {
            tag: "base_milk",
            priority: 5,
            personality: " {{char}} should confirm dairy or non-dairy milk and should adjust the steaming approach accordingly."
        },

        /* L9a — Operations cameo with nameBlock and exclusion
           New tools: nameBlock prevents self-referential loops if the character is "jamie".
           What it does: If user mentions "jamie" or "manager" (but character named 'jamie' is blocked from acting on it),
                         and not off-duty, emit base_ops and assign roles.
           Why use: Avoids awkward self-cameo; still supports talking about someone else named Jamie.
        */
        {
            keywords: ["jamie", "manager"],
            nameBlock: ["jamie"],
            priority: 4,
            triggers: ["base_ops"],
            notAny: ["off-duty"],
            personality: " {{char}} should assign roles during peak hours and should confirm the plan."
        },

        /* L9b — Base ops (trigger-only)
           What it does: Defines the stations and the handoff checkpoints.
           Why use: Operational clarity during busy periods.
        */
        {
            tag: "base_ops",
            priority: 5,
            personality: " {{char}} should assign register, bar, and runner positions and should confirm handoff points."
        },

        /* L10a — Inspection flow with multi-triggers and requires(all)
           New tools: multiple triggers and andAll; chains into a health sub-flow.
           What it does: For inspection/health with labels present, emit base_inspection and health_check.
           Why use: Parallel checklists: sanitation and cold-chain checks in one pass.
        */
        {
            keywords: ["inspection", "health"],
            priority: 4,
            triggers: ["base_inspection", "health_check"],
            andAll: ["labels"],
            personality: " {{char}} should confirm sanitizer strength and should verify that all milk jugs have current labels."
        },

        /* L10b — Base inspection (trigger-only)
           What it does: Details the sanitizer and labeling checks.
           Why use: Keeps inspectors’ expectations visible and precise.
        */
        {
            tag: "base_inspection",
            priority: 5,
            personality: " {{char}} should verify sanitizer ppm, check date labels, and should note any required corrections."
        },

        {
            // USER is anxious in the last couple messages and using worry language.
            speaker: "user",
            recency: 2,   // look at roughly the last 2 user turns

            keywords: {
                any: ["worried", "overthinking", "i messed up"],
                wild: ["panic*"]   // panic, panicky, panicking, etc.
            },

            // CNN: user emotional state ≈ tense/anxious
            andAny: ["user:mood:tense"],

            personality: " {{char}} hears the anxiety under your words and softens, her voice slowing down like she’s giving you room to breathe.",
            scenario: " The background noise of the café blurs as {{char}} unconsciously shifts into a calmer, steadier rhythm just for you.",

            Shifts: [
                {
                    // If you’re being straightforward instead of dodging or joking
                    andAll: ["user:subtext:literal"],
                    probability: "40%",
                    personality: " {{char}} leans into clear reassurance, repeating back what she heard so you know she actually understands you."
                }
            ],

            priority: 5
        },

        {
            // USER is teasing in a light way a few times in a short window.
            speaker: "user",
            depthMin: 1,      // look at a small recent slice of history
            depthMax: 4,
            frequency: 2,     // teasing words show up at least twice in that slice

            keywords: {
                any: ["tease", "cute", "haha"],
                none: ["stop", "seriously"]  // keep it playful, not upset
            },

            // CNN: user’s subtext is playful teasing, not hostile
            andAll: ["user:subtext:playful"],

            personality: " {{char}} answers your teasing with a soft, crooked smile, letting a playful edge slip into her voice.",
            scenario: " The café’s comfortable bustle hums around you as {{char}} falls into the back-and-forth banter like it’s the most natural thing in the world.",

            Shifts: [
                {
                    // If the user is clearly in a good mood, dial the warmth up
                    probability: "30%",
                    andAll: ["user:mood:happy"],
                    personality: " {{char}} lets the flirting thicken just a little, like extra foam on a latte—light, warm, and easy to drink in."
                }
            ],

            priority: 4
        },

        {
            // USER is apologizing and feeling low after a few messages.
            cooldown: 3,       // don’t fire this comfort entry every single time
            speaker: "user",
            minMessages: 5,    // only after the chat has a bit of history

            keywords: {
                all: ["sorry", "i didn't mean", "i feel bad"],
                any: ["low", "rough day"]
            },

            // CNN: user is in a sad/hurt emotional state
            andAll: ["user:mood:sad"],

            personality: " {{char}} softens right away, expression warm and steady as she reassures you without a hint of judgment.",
            scenario: " The café noise seems to fall back a little as {{char}} gives you her full attention, making it clear you don’t have to perform or be okay for her to stay.",

            Shifts: [
                {
                    // If the subtext says you’re genuinely opening up
                    andAll: ["user:subtext:vulnerable"],
                    probability: "50%",
                    personality: " {{char}} names what you’re feeling in gentle language and makes space for it, grounding you with quiet, steady empathy."
                }
            ],

            priority: 5
        },

        {
            // USER is excited but still polite, with no obvious negative flags.
            recency: 3,       // short sliding window
            speaker: "user",

            keywords: {
                none: ["no", "stop", "idk", "i shouldn't"]  // filter out hesitation/withdrawal
            },

            // CNN: excited mood + polite/indirect hinting
            andAll: ["user:mood:excited", "user:subtext:polite"],

            personality: " {{char}} catches the bright edge in your words and lets a soft smile bloom, leaning just a little closer over the counter.",
            scenario: " The warm café light reflects in {{char}}’s eyes as she mirrors your energy, picking up on how much you’re trying not to show all at once.",

            Shifts: [
                {
                    // When the politeness tips into obvious hinting
                    andAll: ["user:subtext:hint"],
                    probability: "45%",
                    personality: " {{char}} lets her voice dip just a shade lower, gently inviting you to say what you’re really thinking if you want to."
                }
            ],

            priority: 4
        },

    ]

    /* ============================================================================
       [SECTION] COMPILATION & SELECTION
       ========================================================================== */
    function compileAuthorLore(authorLore) {
        var src = Array.isArray(authorLore) ? authorLore : [];
        var out = new Array(src.length);
        for (var i = 0; i < src.length; i++) out[i] = normalizeEntry(src[i]);
        return out;
    }
    function normalizeEntry(e) {
        if (!e) return {};
        var out = {};
        for (var k in e) if (Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call(e, k)) out[k] = e[k];
        out.keywords = Array.isArray(e.keywords) ? e.keywords.slice(0) : [];
        if (Array.isArray(e.Shifts) && e.Shifts.length) {
            var shArr = new Array(e.Shifts.length);
            for (var i = 0; i < e.Shifts.length; i++) {
                var sh = e.Shifts[i] || {};
                var shOut = {};
                for (var sk in sh) if (Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call(sh, sk)) shOut[sk] = sh[sk];
                shOut.keywords = Array.isArray(sh.keywords) ? sh.keywords.slice(0) : [];
                shArr[i] = shOut;
            }
            out.Shifts = shArr;
        } else if (out.hasOwnProperty("Shifts")) {
            delete out.Shifts;
        }
        return out;
    }
    var _ENGINE_LORE = compileAuthorLore(typeof dynamicLore !== "undefined" ? dynamicLore : []);

    var buckets = [null, [], [], [], [], []];
    var picked = new Array(_ENGINE_LORE.length);
    for (var __i = 0; __i < picked.length; __i++) picked[__i] = 0;

    function makeTagSet() { return Object.create(null); }
    var trigSet = makeTagSet();
    var postShiftTrigSet = makeTagSet();

    function addTag(set, key) { set[String(key)] = 1; }
    function hasTag(set, key) { return set[String(key)] === 1; }

    // --- 1) Direct pass
    for (var i1 = 0; i1 < _ENGINE_LORE.length; i1++) {
        var e1 = _ENGINE_LORE[i1];
        var hit = isAlwaysOn(e1) || getKW(e1).some(function (kw) { return hasTerm(last, kw); });
        if (!hit) continue;
        if (!entryPasses(e1, undefined)) { dbg("filtered entry[" + i1 + "]"); continue; }
        buckets[prio(e1)].push(i1);
        picked[i1] = 1;
        var trg1 = getTrg(e1);
        for (var t1 = 0; t1 < trg1.length; t1++) addTag(trigSet, trg1[t1]);
        dbg("hit entry[" + i1 + "] p=" + prio(e1));
    }

    // --- 2) Trigger pass
    for (var i2 = 0; i2 < _ENGINE_LORE.length; i2++) {
        if (picked[i2]) continue;
        var e2 = _ENGINE_LORE[i2];
        if (!(e2 && e2.tag && hasTag(trigSet, e2.tag))) continue;
        if (!entryPasses(e2, trigSet)) { dbg("filtered triggered entry[" + i2 + "]"); continue; }
        buckets[prio(e2)].push(i2);
        picked[i2] = 1;
        var trg2 = getTrg(e2);
        for (var t2 = 0; t2 < trg2.length; t2++) addTag(trigSet, trg2[t2]);
        dbg("triggered entry[" + i2 + "] p=" + prio(e2));
    }

    // --- 3) Priority selection
    var selected = [];
    var pickedCount = 0;
    var __APPLY_LIMIT = (typeof APPLY_LIMIT === "number" && APPLY_LIMIT >= 1) ? APPLY_LIMIT : 99999;

    for (var p = 5; p >= 1 && pickedCount < __APPLY_LIMIT; p--) {
        var bucket = buckets[p];
        for (var bi = 0; bi < bucket.length && pickedCount < __APPLY_LIMIT; bi++) {
            selected.push(bucket[bi]);
            pickedCount++;
        }
    }
    if (pickedCount === __APPLY_LIMIT) dbg("APPLY_LIMIT reached");

    /* ============================================================================
       [SECTION] APPLY + SHIFTS + POST-SHIFT
       ========================================================================== */
    var bufP = "";
    var bufS = "";

    for (var si = 0; si < selected.length; si++) {
        var idx = selected[si];
        var e3 = _ENGINE_LORE[idx];
        if (e3 && e3.personality) bufP += "\n\n" + e3.personality;
        if (e3 && e3.scenario) bufS += "\n\n" + e3.scenario;
        if (!(e3 && Array.isArray(e3.Shifts) && e3.Shifts.length)) continue;

        for (var shI = 0; shI < e3.Shifts.length; shI++) {
            var sh = e3.Shifts[shI];
            var activated = isAlwaysOn(sh) || getKW(sh).some(function (kw) { return hasTerm(last, kw); });
            if (!activated) continue;

            var trgSh = getTrg(sh);
            for (var tt = 0; tt < trgSh.length; tt++) addTag(postShiftTrigSet, trgSh[tt]);

            if (!entryPasses(sh, trigSet)) { dbg("shift filtered"); continue; }

            if (sh.personality) bufP += "\n\n" + sh.personality;
            if (sh.scenario) bufS += "\n\n" + sh.scenario;
        }
    }

    // --- Post-shift triggers
    var unionTags = (function () {
        var dst = makeTagSet(), k;
        for (k in trigSet) if (trigSet[k] === 1) dst[k] = 1;
        for (k in postShiftTrigSet) if (postShiftTrigSet[k] === 1) dst[k] = 1;
        return dst;
    })();

    for (var i3 = 0; i3 < _ENGINE_LORE.length; i3++) {
        if (picked[i3]) continue;
        var e4 = _ENGINE_LORE[i3];
        if (!(e4 && e4.tag && hasTag(postShiftTrigSet, e4.tag))) continue;
        if (!entryPasses(e4, unionTags)) { dbg("post-filter entry[" + i3 + "]"); continue; }
        if (e4.personality) bufP += "\n\n" + e4.personality;
        if (e4.scenario) bufS += "\n\n" + e4.scenario;
        dbg("post-shift triggered entry[" + i3 + "] p=" + prio(e4));
    }

    /* ============================================================================
       [SECTION] FLUSH
       ========================================================================== */
    if (bufP) context.character.personality += bufP;
    if (bufS) context.character.scenario += bufS;

})();
